Yes, the bridge code is compiling; no, that alone does not prove the bridge will work end-to-end.

Why this matters: compile success means the code builds, but bridge correctness depends on runtime integration (Bor + Heimdall + contracts + finalized event flow), not just compilation.

Facts (confirmed):
- Last build pass was clean for `bridge/pos-portal`, `bridge/pos-contracts`, `bridge/genesis-contracts`, `bridge/bor`, and `bridge/heimdall-v2`.
- Since that check, changes were docs-only (no bridge runtime code changes).

Inference (likely cause):
- “Will it work” is still unproven because no end-to-end bridge execution test was run in this answer-only step.

Actions (recommended next steps):
1. Run an end-to-end bridge smoke test (deposit -> checkpoint/finality -> mint/unlock path) on your target environment.
2. Verify finalized-event gating and balance/accounting invariants (`GOLD`/`GILT`) during that flow.

Completion level: complete (answer-only).
